title: rubus hirtus var. pachyphylloides
intro: Rubus hirtus var. pachyphylloides is a variant of the Rubus hirtus species, a type of wild raspberry or bramble native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional European herbal medicine. The leaves and roots of Rubus hirtus var. pachyphylloides contain tannins and flavonoids, which have been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, contributing to various health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting digestive health. Rubus hirtus var. pachyphylloides is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in jams and preserves, although its use should be in moderation and under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
